one_liner: SpaceX's record $75B IPO trades today amid a surging equity supply wave, Anthropic is alienating partners and customers (including ours), and energy-driven inflation stays hot.

top_themes.headline: SpaceX debut caps a surge in equity supply

points.text: SpaceX priced its IPO at $135, raising $75B at a ~$1.77T valuation; shares start trading on Nasdaq today as SPCX.
details: Demand was 4x the 555.6M shares offered; retail alone submitted >$100B in orders.
At the IPO price, SpaceX is worth ~60% of Microsoft's market cap on a fraction of the revenue.

points.text: New equity issuance is at near-record levels and could erode the buyback-driven supply tailwind that has supported the market for two decades.
details: Fed data: $389B of new equity hit the market in Q1, the largest quarter ex the 2021 SPAC froth.
AI capex is the driver — Oracle and Alphabet both plan large stock sales; mega-cap buybacks are slowing.
Goldman argues supply is still modest relative to total market cap and won't derail the bull market.

points.text: A heavy IPO pipeline (Anthropic, OpenAI, Sierra Space, Axiom) raises the question of whether retail money is a finite resource.
details: Vanda flagged three straight days of retail net selling of single stocks, first time since March 2020.
JPMorgan sees the selling as loss-cutting on tech weakness rather than raising cash for IPOs.

top_themes.headline: Anthropic turns on its partners and customers

points.text: Anthropic blindsided Figma and Canva by repositioning Claude Design to compete directly with them days before launch; Figma pulled out and Krieger left Figma's board.
Claude Fable adoption is being slowed by Anthropic's new 30-day data retention policy, which is deterring enterprise customers including Microsoft and UiPath.
details: Retained data can sit outside customers' configured cloud, raising GDPR and security questions.
Anthropic is effectively raising prices on Fable even as customers hesitate.

points.text: OpenAI is weighing significant price cuts to take share from Anthropic, per WSJ — a model-layer price war is forming.
Anthropic is simultaneously scaling enterprise distribution, partnering with TCS for deployments.

top_themes.headline: AI infrastructure reality check; TSMC lock loosens

points.text: Nvidia's scale-up roadmap is slipping: NVL144 cancelled/delayed, NVL576 at risk, and CPO inflection pushed toward 2029.
details: Demand is intact — pluggable 1.6T is very strong and NPO is snowballing among non-Nvidia hyperscalers.
800VDC single-ended is pushed to 2028+; ±400VDC stays on track for 2H26.

points.text: Google ordered 3M+ TPUs from Intel for 2028 and Nvidia is evaluating Intel 18A — real second-sourcing away from a capacity-constrained TSMC.
details: Intel jumped ~13% premarket on the report and closed +9.3% after a BofA upgrade.

points.text: The AI buildout is causing a structural DRAM/NAND shortage that is upending corporate IT budgets.
Capital keeps flooding in: KKR and Nvidia are launching a $10B data center financing company.
CPI ran 4.2% y/y through May, the fastest since April 2023, driven by Iran-war energy prices; real wages are now flat since January 2025.
details: Inflation looks contained to energy-related categories so far.

points.text: The ECB hiked 25bps to keep the energy spike out of core, but Lagarde signaled no further hikes this summer.
Risk rallied anyway: S&P 500 closed +1.8% as Trump said the U.S. and Iran are close to a deal to extend the ceasefire and reopen the Strait of Hormuz.

positions.name: Meta Platforms
positions.notes: The Information (11 sources inside the company): six months post-close, the Rivos acquisition is failing to accelerate Meta's in-house AI chip effort — strategy churn, shifting leadership priorities, and tensions between Rivos and the incumbent chips team. Separately, Meta cut operational ties with Manus, and SemiAnalysis confirms Meta is deploying ±400VDC for VR200 racks with Lite-On as a third sidecar vendor.
positions.claude_take: The custom-silicon cost-curve story is slipping right as Google scales TPUs to a second foundry. That means Meta stays a price-taker on Nvidia for longer, so capex efficiency and incremental ROIC on the AI buildout lag the bull case. Ad revenue strength has to carry the spend; watch for any sign management resets the in-house silicon timeline at the next print.

positions.name: Microsoft
positions.notes: Microsoft is among the enterprises holding off on internal use of Claude Fable over Anthropic's new 30-day data retention policy, which stores customer inputs — sometimes outside the customer's configured cloud boundary. Side note: SpaceX's $1.75T IPO valuation equals ~60% of MSFT's market cap, a marker of how much mega-cap money the new supply wave could compete for.
positions.claude_take: The data-governance friction at Anthropic is a free advert for Azure and Copilot's enterprise-trust pitch — first-party models inside the customer's compliance boundary. It reinforces Microsoft's distribution moat with regulated buyers and supports pricing on the AI stack. The model-layer price war (OpenAI cutting to fight Anthropic) lowers Microsoft's input costs over time.

positions.name: Amazon
positions.notes: AWS explicitly warned customers that opting into Anthropic's Fable data retention means data 'will leave AWS's data and security boundary' — notable given AWS both hosts Anthropic and resells its models. Separately, SemiAnalysis flags accelerating ARM server adoption including Graviton.
positions.claude_take: Near-term friction: the retention issue could slow Fable consumption on Bedrock. But AWS drawing a hard security boundary protects its core enterprise trust moat, which matters more than one model cycle. Graviton momentum extends AWS's unit-cost advantage and margin durability as AI workloads scale.

positions.name: London Stock Exchange Group
positions.notes: Axios Pro Rata flags another company abandoning a London listing — the LSE attrition story continues even as global issuance booms and the record SpaceX IPO lands on Nasdaq.
positions.claude_take: Listings are a small slice of revenue; the thesis rests on data, analytics, and Workspace, which are unaffected. But persistent listing losses are a slow bleed to the franchise's ecosystem and a recurring sentiment overhang on the stock. The global primary-issuance wave bypassing London underlines that capital formation has moved to the US.

one_liner: Hot CPI and a rate-sensitive tape dominate the macro picture, while Anthropic's Fable 5 launch reshapes the enterprise AI cost and data-security debate that touches our software names.

points.text: May CPI rose 4.2% year-on-year, the fastest since April 2023, driven by energy from the Iran war.
details: Real wages for production workers are up just 0.1% since January 2025.
ECB raised rates 0.25pt but signalled no further summer hikes.

points.text: Stocks have become acutely sensitive to rising Treasury yields.
details: Nasdaq 100's 4.8% drop last Friday was its worst day since the April 2025 tariff panic.
S&P 500 down 3% and Nasdaq 100 down over 5% from June 2 highs.
Morgan Stanley's Wilson sees the Fed staying accommodative through the midterms.

top_themes.headline: Anthropic Fable 5 launch and AI price war

points.text: Anthropic released Claude Fable 5, a Mythos-class model, with strong coding gains but stricter guardrails.
details: Priced at $10/M input and $50/M output tokens, double the Opus models.
Stripe migrated a 50-million-line codebase in a day using Fable.
Cybersecurity and biology queries are routed down to weaker Opus 4.8.

points.text: A new 30-day data retention policy is giving enterprise customers pause.
details: AWS warned data leaves its security boundary once retention is enabled.
UiPath is reviewing GDPR exposure before adopting Fable internally.

points.text: OpenAI is weighing drastic token price cuts to take share from Anthropic.
details: A price war would test both labs' margins ahead of anticipated IPOs.
Anthropic filed IPO paperwork; the 'subsidy era' is widely seen as ending.

top_themes.headline: AI power and datacenter capacity crunch

points.text: US grid headroom turns negative by 2027, pushing datacenters behind-the-meter.
details: SemiAnalysis sees over half of new datacenters powered by onsite gas in 2028.
Bloom Energy and reciprocating engines flagged as key beneficiaries.

points.text: Hyperscaler leasing remains very strong; 2026 datacenter leasing could reach 30GW.
details: Microsoft leased nearly 1GW in Q2 in the US and is building a 2GW European region for OpenAI and Anthropic.
Meta expanding its Prometheus cluster to 3GW.
Broadcom, Apollo and Blackstone launching a $35bn AI infrastructure platform.

points.text: SpaceX IPO is more than four times oversubscribed and prices tonight, trading tomorrow.
details: 555.6m shares at $135 each; set to be the largest IPO ever.
Index funds may eye up to 30% of the float, stressing passive mechanisms.

positions.name: Microsoft
positions.notes: Microsoft is among Anthropic customers holding off on Fable 5 over the 30-day data retention policy. Separately, The Information reports Xbox is planning layoffs as revenue and margins decline.
positions.claude_take: The Fable data-residency friction is a reminder that Microsoft's enterprise trust and compliance posture is itself a moat; customers default to its boundaries. Xbox weakness is immaterial to the thesis, which rests on Azure and Copilot monetization, not gaming.

positions.name: Amazon
positions.notes: AWS told customers their data leaves its security boundary once Anthropic's Fable retention is enabled. Amazon also expanded its CTV ad advantage, adding LinkedIn data and commerce-based targeting for Netflix inventory.
positions.claude_take: AWS sits at the center of the Anthropic stack, which is a structural tailwind even as data-governance questions surface. The advertising data loop is the more underappreciated earnings driver; tying impressions to purchases deepens a high-margin, defensible business.

positions.name: Workday
positions.notes: No direct news, but the Fable 5 launch and the broader enterprise-AI cost and coding-automation wave bear directly on application software.
positions.claude_take: Rapidly improving coding models raise the long-run question of whether large suite vendors capture or cede AI value. Workday's moat is its system-of-record data and compliance depth, not code, so near-term risk is limited, but pricing power must be watched.

positions.name: SAP
positions.notes: Implicated by the same enterprise-AI shift; coding agents like Fable migrated a 50-million-line codebase in a day.
positions.claude_take: Faster code migration could cut switching costs that protect SAP's installed base over time. For now the ERP data gravity and regulatory entrenchment keep the moat intact, and AI more likely accelerates RISE migrations than displaces them.

positions.name: Toast
positions.notes: No company-specific news, but a hot CPI print and squeezed real wages pressure consumer-facing restaurant spend.
positions.claude_take: Stalled real wages and energy-driven inflation are a demand headwind for restaurant volumes and Toast's payment-linked revenue. The fintech attach and software stickiness should cushion this, but watch same-store sales and net location adds.
